## [00:00 - 03:00]

In this lesson, I'm going to show you how to build long lasting relationships with influencers that you work with. Creating strong working relationships with influencers can be hugely advantageous to the extent that some influencers may even end up reducing their rates on working with you or ultimately posting for free. The way that I go about this is by using my sweetener framework. In it, there are three main factors that will build or sweeten the influencer brand relationship. The first is that you want to make the influencer feel included in your brand story and vision as well as the problem that you're trying to solve. Here, you'd clue them into your brand's why. This is why we do what we do and how your work will help boost the brand mission. Make sure the influencer feels the value and importance of their role and knows exactly where they fit in place in the grand scheme of your mission. This is particularly powerful if your brand or campaign has a social cause or charitable aspect to it. This is because it makes them feel a part of something larger than themselves. The second is you want to make the influencer feel special. Tell your influencer why you chose them over others, how you do things differently and better, and make your communication and collaboration as personalized to them as possible. The last big one is to make the influencer feel like a VIP. Influencers are becoming increasingly used to VIP treatment and this goes beyond just getting free products and into far more customized, personalized experiences. So you want to go above and beyond. Aim to surprise and delight and always exceed expectations. One example of this is when Shopify sent out one of their power users, Kylie Jenner, a customized jumping castle for her birthday party. This might seem crazy but if you think about it, Kylie Jenner costs upwards of $300,000 a post and the jumping castle would have cost around $20,000 tops. So it's actually a really smart move as she did end up posting about it. Of course there's a bit of risk involved in bigger showy public displays like this as they may not end up posting it or even find it too much. So make sure that you do your research first. So clearly some of these ideas will only be relevant when working with highly paid macro influencers as they could be a bit costly and resource intensive to coordinate with micro influencers. Some more ideas include at the very least including a handwritten thank you card with the product when it's sent out, product customization and personalization like having their name printed engraved or embroidered onto your product, creating influencer only products or brand merchandise. For example, if you're a bikini company and it's winter, gift your influencer a branded fluffy robe and slippers to say we get it. It's cold shooting in a bikini. Even creating a gift pack

## [03:00 - 04:13]

around your product with complimentary products from other non-competing brands within your niche or it could just be sending them a big bunch of flowers for no reason. And then on the more extreme end including an all-expenses paid experience in your campaign like a trip to New York or Bali or the increasingly popular branded influencer retreats concept. So just have a think, look at what others are doing and get creative. You also want to find relevant reasons to make your influencers feel special and connected to your brand. So another idea is helping them celebrate a big milestone like their birthday, graduating university or a special anniversary with thoughtful gifts and experiences. So I'd make sure to have a running list of important events in your influencer's life. A good rule to keep in mind is that you want to treat your close influencer relationships like you treat your close personal relationships at work. But remember it's still a working relationship at the same time so always keep a level of professionalism. As an action item for this lesson, decide on the ways that you'll make your influencers feel a valuable party of tribe. So you've reached the end of this module. I hope you found a lot of value and it's time for me to pass you on to the next instructor.
